customer_first_recommendation.selection_rule: Choose the highest developer-revenue shortlist band that still keeps customer savings positive.
customer_first_recommendation.why_this_band: This is the strongest shortlist band that still protects the customer from paying above the EVN benchmark in the simple screening view.

commercial_candidate_memo.decision_summary: Advance `5% below ceiling` as the primary commercial candidate; keep `ceiling` only as fallback and discard the premium band.
commercial_candidate_memo.customer_first_reason: This is the strongest shortlist band that still protects the customer from paying above the EVN benchmark in the simple screening view.

candidates.rationale: Best customer-first option: preserves positive customer savings while still keeping strong developer revenue.
Commercially viable fallback with lower customer risk, but weaker customer savings upside than the recommended band.
Creates explicit customer premium, so it conflicts with the accepted customer-first framing.

review_endpoint.question: Should the team advance the recommended customer-first band into commercial discussion, while holding parity as fallback and discarding the premium band?
review_endpoint.decision_artifact: Review commercial_candidate_memo together with customer_first_recommendation to confirm the advance / hold / discard shortlist.
